title: Songadh Fort
tagline: Songadh Fort — heritage city of Gujarat

overview.short: Songadh fort is a 16th-century fort in Songadh town of Tapi district, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: Songadh fort is a 16th-century fort in Songadh town of Tapi district, Gujarat, India. The fort gots its name from the Gujarati language terms son (gold) and gadh (fort). It is located near the Tapi River Ukai Dam at an elevation of 112 meters above sea level.Thingle Sardar of Raigan Subhya of Nawapur captured this fort during the Uprising of 1857 with the Bhil army while marching towards Surat but was stopped here by the British forces.

topPlaces.name: Songadh
topPlaces.description: Songadh is a taluka in Tapi district in the Indian state of Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Vekur
topPlaces.description: Vekur is a village in Tapi district of Gujarat state of India.

topPlaces.name: Ukai Thermal Power Station
topPlaces.description: Ukai Thermal Power Station of the Gujarat State Electricity Corporation Limited, India, is a power station with an installed capacity of 1,110 MW. It is one of Gujarat's major coal-fired power plants, located on the bank of the Tapi river.

topPlaces.name: Ukai
topPlaces.description: Ukai is a census town in Tapi district in the Indian state of Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Ukai Dam
topPlaces.description: The Ukai Dam, constructed across the Tapti River, is the second largest reservoir in Gujarat, India, after the Sardar Sarovar. It is also known as Vallabh Sagar.

items.note: Songadh is a taluka in Tapi district in the Indian state of Gujarat.
Vekur is a village in Tapi district of Gujarat state of India.

Ukai Thermal Power Station of the Gujarat State Electricity Corporation Limited, India, is a power station with an installed capacity of 1,110 MW. It is one of Gujarat's major coal-fired power plants, located on the bank of the Tapi river.
Ukai is a census town in Tapi district in the Indian state of Gujarat.
The Ukai Dam, constructed across the Tapti River, is the second largest reservoir in Gujarat, India, after the Sardar Sarovar. It is also known as Vallabh Sagar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Songadh Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Songadh Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Songadh Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Surat Airport (84 km) and the nearest railway station is Surat Railway Station (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Songadh Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Songadh Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Songadh Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Songadh Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Songadh Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Songadh, Vekur, Ukai Thermal Power Station, Ukai, Ukai Dam.
